sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.26 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: (Shi Shengming). Is it true that a finite$p 0.1ex$-soluble group$G$has a$p 0.1ex$-block of defect zero if and only if there exists an element$x O_{p'}(G)$such that$C_G(x)$is a$p'$-sub\-group? { Jiping Zhang} No, it is not. The group$3^2 . GL_2(3)$has a 2-block of defect$0$, but the centralizer of every element from$O(G)$has even order (V.\,I.\,Zenkov, in: Trudy Inst. Matem. i Mekh. UrO RAN, { 3} (...
